title: For the Lonely
scripture_text: I will never leave you nor forsake you.
reflection: Loneliness is not the same as aloneness. The Lord was alone on the cross so that you would never have to be.
body:
Tonight, the house is quiet. The phone is not ringing. The friends are busy, or the friends are gone. The longing is sharp, and old, and very tired.

You are not the only one. There are more lonely people tonight than there are accompanied ones. This is the strange, secret math of the world.

The Lord is with you. He is with you in the silence, in the way no human can be. He knows your name. He knows the specific shape of your loneliness. He is closer than the next room.

You may not feel Him tonight. That is okay. Feelings are not the measure. He is there.

Tomorrow, reach out to one person. Send one text. Make one call. The loneliness is real, and the cure is real, and it often starts with you.
prayer: Lord, I am lonely. Sit with me. And help me to reach out tomorrow. Amen.
